acceptance_criteria.given: a legacy grant at /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json (pattern 'git push', is_regex false) and a matching sentinel at /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json (op 'git', args_contain ['push'])
acceptance_criteria.when: posttool-allowlist-consume.py receives a Bash PostToolUse stdin payload with agent_id present and tool_response.exit_code=0; tested twice: once with command 'git push' (single space) and once with command 'git   push' (two spaces, whitespace-normalization case)
acceptance_criteria.then: both the legacy grant file and the sentinel file are absent after the hook runs in both whitespace variants, and the hook exits 0 in both cases

assert: NOTE: the whitespace-normalization variant is the critical new regression — 'git   push' (two spaces) must also unlink the legacy grant even though literal substring match 'git push' NOT in 'git   push'
check.note: The 4th edit in posttool-allowlist-consume.py ensures that whenever the sentinel is consumed (tokenized match), the legacy grant is also unconditionally unlinked — closing the divergence between tokenized sentinel matching and literal-substring legacy matching

acceptance_criteria.when: posttool-allowlist-consume.py receives a Bash PostToolUse stdin payload WITHOUT agent_id, matching command, and tool_response.exit_code=0
acceptance_criteria.then: the legacy grant file is absent after the hook runs and the hook exits 0 (regression: main-agent behavior preserved)

acceptance_criteria.given: a UserPromptSubmit payload with agent_id present
acceptance_criteria.then: neither /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json nor /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json is written; hook exits 0 (write firewall Step 0 preserved)

check.note: Write firewall is in hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0 (lines 17-23), NOT in pretool-bash-safety.sh

acceptance_criteria.given: a matching sentinel at /tmp/claude-grants/<task_id>.json (no legacy grant)
acceptance_criteria.when: posttool-allowlist-consume.py receives a Bash PostToolUse payload with agent_id present; tested for both exit_code=0 and exit_code=1
acceptance_criteria.then: sentinel file is absent after the hook runs in both cases (consume-on-any-terminal-result preserved)

assert: sentinel grant file absent for exit_code=0
sentinel grant file absent for exit_code=1

acceptance_criteria.given: no legacy grant file present (absent), a valid sentinel present
acceptance_criteria.when: posttool-allowlist-consume.py receives a Bash PostToolUse payload with agent_id present
acceptance_criteria.then: hook exits 0, no traceback in stderr, sentinel is consumed normally (fail-open on absent legacy grant)

acceptance_criteria.given: only a legacy grant at /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json (no sentinel)
acceptance_criteria.when: pretool-bash-safety.sh receives a Bash PreToolUse payload with agent_id present and command matching the legacy grant pattern
acceptance_criteria.then: the hook does NOT allow the command via legacy path; check_and_consume_allowlist returns 1 for subagents (IS_SUBAGENT=1 firewall preserved)

assert: hook does not emit permissionDecision:allow JSON for legacy-only subagent call
legacy grant read path returns 1 (IS_SUBAGENT check in check_and_consume_allowlist)
check.note: This AC verifies the fix does NOT widen PreTool subagent authorization; only PostTool cleanup is affected
